title: Finding your place in the story of Jesus. Part 4
scripture_text: ‘’Now Jesus himself was about thirty years old when he began his ministry. He was the son, so it was thought, of Joseph, the son of Heli, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melki, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph, the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai, the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Josek, the son of Joda, the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri the son of Melki, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmadam, the son of Er, the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, he son of Levi, the son of Simeon the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim, the son of Melea, the son of Menna, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David, the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon, the son of Amminadab, the son of Ram the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah, the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor, the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah, the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech, the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalalel, the son of Kenan, the son of Enosh, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.’’
devotional:
The genealogy of Jesus, teaches us that God uses the lives of individuals to accomplish his sovereign purposes in history. As we have said, the most significant thing about any of us, is the part we play in the story of Jesus. We may, or may not know what part we are playing. Many in the genealogy had no idea that they were playing a part in Jesus coming into the world. God was using their lives to ensure that the glory of his sovereign plans would be revealed.

The genealogy of Jesus shouts ‘Your life is about much more than you!’

Our lives are about God and his continual involvement in history. That does not mean that God just sees us as inanimate objects like pieces on a chess board that he moves at his whim. Rather we are his co-workers, his ‘instruments of righteousness’ that tell his story to this present generation.

The first name that Luke mentions in this chapter is Tiberius, who’s full name was Tiberius Caesar Augustus son of god. For him to have this title meant he had to be born into a royal family with pomp, power and prestige.

Jesus also came from a royal family, but at the time of his birth there was no pomp, power and prestige. Jesus the Son of God was born into the ordinary, making the ordinary extraordinary.
prayer: Father I confess that most of the time I am unaware of my part in Jesus’ story. Help me to be more aware of this fact in my daily life.
html:
Finding your place in the story of Jesus. Part 4

Study Text: Luke 3:23-38
‘’Now Jesus himself was about thirty years old when he began his ministry. He was the son, so it was thought, of Joseph, the son of Heli, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melki, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph, the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai, the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Josek, the son of Joda, the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri the son of Melki, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmadam, the son of Er, the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, he son of Levi, the son of Simeon the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim, the son of Melea, the son of Menna, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David, the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon, the son of Amminadab, the son of Ram the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah, the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor, the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah, the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech, the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalalel, the son of Kenan, the son of Enosh, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.’’

The genealogy of Jesus, teaches us that God uses the lives of individuals to accomplish his sovereign purposes in history. As we have said, the most significant thing about any of us, is the part we play in the story of Jesus. We may, or may not know what part we are playing. Many in the genealogy had no idea that they were playing a part in Jesus coming into the world. God was using their lives to ensure that the glory of his sovereign plans would be revealed.

The genealogy of Jesus shouts ‘Your life is about much more than you!’

Our lives are about God and his continual involvement in history. That does not mean that God just sees us as inanimate objects like pieces on a chess board that he moves at his whim. Rather we are his co-workers, his ‘instruments of righteousness’ that tell his story to this present generation.

The first name that Luke mentions in this chapter is Tiberius, who’s full name was Tiberius Caesar Augustus son of god. For him to have this title meant he had to be born into a royal family with pomp, power and prestige.

Jesus also came from a royal family, but at the time of his birth there was no pomp, power and prestige. Jesus the Son of God was born into the ordinary, making the ordinary extraordinary.

Prayer: Father I confess that most of the time I am unaware of my part in Jesus’ story. Help me to be more aware of this fact in my daily life.
Bible in a year reading plan: - Job 19-21
